    14	## Locked estimator decisions

    16	These are preconditions, not choices left to an executing worker.

1	# Result dependency and rerun map
     2	
     3	**Routing instructions only.** This file defines dependency and invalidation
     4	rules; it does not record current completion. Live work belongs in
     5	`OPEN_ITEMS.md` and dimensional STATUS files, verified numbers in
     6	`../VALIDATION_LEDGER.md`, bugs in `../KNOWN_ISSUES.md`, and chronology in the
     7	RUN_LOGs.
     8	
     9	## Node vocabulary
    10	
    11	| Mark | Meaning |
    12	|---|---|
    13	| `REUSE` | Consume only after its commit, summary, content fingerprint, and estimator contract validate. |
    14	| `BUILD` | A new node is required in a distinct namespace. |
    15	| `REPLACE` | Swap only the named component; prove all other component hashes are unchanged. |
    16	| `QUARANTINE` | Preserve as a labeled control/cross-check; never feed a publication descendant. |
    17	| `GATED` | No launch until all named upstream commits and validators pass. |
    18	
    19	No filesystem artifact is implicitly `REUSE`. Size-only existence checks never
    20	establish completion.
    21	
    22	## Dependency graph
    23

    62	## Estimator identities and allowed parents
    63	
    64	| Publication node | Required identity | Allowed parents | Forbidden parents |
    65	|---|---|---|---|

    71	
    72	## Scalar 5D and corrected 4D routes
    73	
    74	```text
    75	REUSE validated scalar 5D central + corrected non-lateral components
    76	  + BUILD P3S selection-complete standard endpoints
    77	  -> REPLACE standard lateral only
    78	  -> ADOPT final scalar 5D covariance
    79	  -> PROJECT lower-dimensional covariances and significances
    80	
    81	REUSE corrected R1 independent-4D central + non-lateral components

    84	  -> ADOPT independent 4D covariance
    85	```
    86	
    87	The R1 4D throws and other corrected non-lateral components remain reusable
    88	unless their own code/config/input contract changes. A selection-complete
    89	lateral change does not authorize rerunning them. Publication may instead use

    93	## Scalar FPS route
    94	
    95	```text
    96	VALIDATE reusable FPS event-loop/universe inputs
    97	  -> BUILD explicit negweight-refined central and endpoint unfolds
    98	     in a mode-stamped atomic namespace
    99	  -> BUILD/VALIDATE compatible statistical, ML, vertical, joint-throw,

   101	  -> ADOPT scalar FPS covariance
   102	```
   103	
   104	All existing purity-footed unfolds, central values, and covariances are
   105	`QUARANTINE` matched controls. Reusing an underlying ROOT input requires proof
   106	that it contains the background and universe information needed to construct

   108	
   109	## Publication PET route
   110	
   111	```text
   112	PG0 controls committed
   113	  -> binary-owner drain gate
   114	  -> G2 full-schema FPS CV ROOT/NPZ with data, signal, truth, and background
   115	     clouds/scalars/keys/weights
   116	  -> freeze pet-fullevent-fps-v1 fingerprint
   117	  -> BUILD literal signed data+background target and Stay-Positive refinement
   118	  -> BUILD nominal + GPU floor
   119	  -> BUILD F7 coherent C_stat over full data/signal/background inventories
   120	  + BUILD PET C_ML with no Poisson variation
   121	  + BUILD joint physical-variation-plus-retraining vertical/flux components
   122	  + BUILD P3F-PET full-schema shifted inputs and joint lateral retraining

   124	  -> PROJECT/report acceptance-supported and prior-dominated tiers
   125	```
   126	
   127	G2 precedes both the nominal and fresh full-schema P3F-PET. Reduced P3F or
   128	recoil-only `xps2` artifacts cannot be promoted by changing a label.
   129	
   130	For every PET nuisance that can change the learned mapping,
   131	
   132	```text
   133	delta_u = x_u(varied physical input + retrained estimator) - x_CV
   134	```
   135	
   136	and the component is built directly from the declared set of joint shifts. Do
   137	not form a separate additive frozen-map covariance plus retraining covariance
   138	for the same nuisance. Per-universe background target construction repeats the
   139	literal negative injection and Stay-Positive refinement on that universe.
   140	
   141	## F7 coherent statistical route
   142	
   143	For each replica `r`:
   144	
   145	1. Enumerate the full ordered data, signal-MC, and background-MC inventories.
   146	2. Draw and persist coherent Poisson factors before any training subset.
   147	3. Apply data factors to data events, signal factors wherever signal MC enters,
   148	   and background factors to the negative background injection.
   149	4. Run the replica-specific Stay-Positive refinement after those factors.
   150	5. Select the training subset without redrawing or reindexing the global
   151	   factors.
   152	6. Reuse the exact applicable MC/background factors at extraction and prove
   153	   row/key identity.
   154	
   155	The nominal refined target is not a replica input. Missing inventory members,
   156	duplicate keys, seed/factor mismatch, or incomplete extraction fail the whole
   157	replica manifest.
   158	
   159	## Trigger-to-invalidation rules
   160	
   161	| Trigger | Earliest restart | Invalidated descendants | Explicitly unaffected |
   162	|---|---|---|---|
   163	| P3S standard endpoints change | Standard lateral merge | P4-5D/P4-4D lateral and their adoptions/projections | Frozen central and validated non-lateral components |
   164	| Scalar FPS background mode or target changes | P3F-scalar unfold | Scalar FPS central, compatible UQ, adoption, comparisons | Standard scalar chain; legacy recoil PET |
   165	| G2 feature/schema/input changes | G2 CV/interface validation | P5A, P3F-PET, all P5B components and projections | Scalar results; legacy recoil PET |
   166	| PET estimator fingerprint changes | P5A nominal | Floor and all PET UQ/descendants | Scalar results and quarantined cross-checks |
   167	| F7 inventory/draw contract changes | First affected replica | `C_stat`, PET total, PET projections/comparisons | P5A nominal/floor and non-stat components |
   168	| PET nuisance implementation changes | First affected joint universe | Its component, PET total, dependent projections | Other independently owned components after hash proof |
   169	| Projection/bin-volume mapping changes | Projection validator | Projected covariance and derived significances | Upstream adopted covariance |

   175

   184	renames. An interrupted partial is quarantined and regenerated; a nonzero file

   191	block reconstruction, non-mutation of reused components, symmetry, PSD/eigen

    27	
    28	Before any publication PET compute:
    29	
    30	1. The locked background decision, full-event contract, dependency map, and

    38	   resource, dependency, remaining-wall, and output-ownership evidence. No two
    39	   jobs may write one namespace.

    42	
    43	## Gate 1 — G2 full-schema FPS CV input
    44

    46	`pet-fullevent-fps-v1` schema. The source must include aligned data, signal MC,
    47	truth MC, and background MC clouds plus event scalars/features, event keys,
    48	masks/types, POT/weights, native misses, extended-FPS edges, and schema/binary
    49	provenance.

    52	
    53	- `MNV101_FULL_PHASE_SPACE=1` and the declared full-event dump are embedded and
    54	  verified;

    56	- event-key and row alignment, uniqueness, denominator/miss consistency, and
    57	  reconstructed-selection identity;

    59	- no truth-only feature enters step 1;

    68	
    69	Build the measured-side training inventory from:
    70	
    71	- aligned data events with their positive data weights; and
    72	- aligned background clouds with negative POT-scaled `w_bkg`.
    73	
    74	Apply the Stay-Positive refinement to that complete signed inventory. Do not
    75	copy scalar purity weights, silently downweight only the data rows, or reuse a
    76	nominal refined target in a bootstrap replica.
    77	
    78	The target summary records:
    79	
    80	- ordered data and background manifests and event-key alignment;
    81	- POT scale and raw positive/negative sums;

    89	refined targets reproduce their independently constructed binned checks; all
    90	invalid/missing/misaligned background inputs fail closed.
    91	
    92	## Gate 3 — nominal and GPU floor
    93	
    94	Train and extract one unbootstrapped publication nominal from the Gate-2 target
    95	using the frozen estimator fingerprint. Freeze its central vector, reported-bin
    96	mask/order, phase-space edges, seed/config policy, and extraction normalization.
    97	
    98	Run one matched repeat to bound GPU nondeterminism before interpreting either
    99	statistical or ML ensembles.
   100	
   101	Required validation includes ordinary closure, the omitted-muon stress closure,
   102	finite full-coverage weights, strict MC index/order, cap-sensitivity telemetry,
   103	normalization, exact lower-dimensional marginals, and the acceptance-supported
   104	versus prior-dominated reporting split.
   105	
   106	**Gate:** committed nominal and floor packets. No UQ component may use a
   107	different central/mask/order/fingerprint.
   108	
   109	## Gate 4 — F7 coherent statistical replicas
   110	
   111	For every replica, in this exact order:
   112	
   113	1. Enumerate complete, ordered data, signal-MC, and background-MC inventories
   114	   before any subset or distributed slice.
   115	2. Draw one coherent Poisson factor per inventory member from a persisted,
   116	   replayable replica seed policy.
   117	3. Apply data factors to data weights, signal factors everywhere signal MC is
   118	   used, and background factors to the negative background injection.
   119	4. Run a fresh Stay-Positive refinement for that replica after applying the
   120	   background factors.
   121	5. Select the training subset without redrawing, shortening, or reindexing the
   122	   full factors.
   123	6. Reuse the exact applicable signal/background factors during full extraction
   124	   and completeness/count construction.
   125	
   126	Persist full inventory hashes, seeds, factor hashes or replayable factors,
   127	subset indices, train/extract identity checks, target/refinement telemetry, and
   128	completion status. A missing unit invalidates the replica; a missing replica
   129	invalidates the declared ensemble manifest. Center `C_stat` on the accepted
   130	replica mean.
   131	
   132	Run a small pilot only to validate the machinery. Declare the publication
   133	replica inventory before viewing its covariance and compare its spread with the
   134	Gate-3 floor.
   135	
   136	## Gate 5 — PET-specific ML ensemble
   137	
   138	Use the nominal target with no Poisson fluctuation. Vary only the predeclared
   139	crossed training/subsample/split and estimator seeds. Persist all seeds and
   140	completed extractions; center on the ensemble mean and compare with the GPU
   141	floor. Scalar GBDT ML ensembles do not substitute for this component.
   142	
   143	## Gate 6 — targeted joint systematic retraining decision
   144	
   145	Publication vertical/flux systematics are joint end-to-end variations. For a
   146	nuisance `u` that can change the learned mapping:
   147	
   148	```text
   149	delta_u = x_u(varied physical input + universe-specific background refinement
   150	              + retrained estimator) - x_CV
   151	```
   152	
   153	The component is constructed directly from the declared joint shifts. Do not
   154	build or add separate frozen-map and retraining covariances for the same
   155	nuisance; they share the nuisance and would omit/corrupt their cross term.
   156	
   157	First run a predeclared targeted set spanning dominant model, flux, and
   158	kinematic behaviors. Compare the joint retrained displacement with the matched
   159	frozen-map diagnostic only to decide expansion. Declare trace and tail
   160	materiality thresholds before looking at results and bound the GPU floor.
   161	
   162	- If the learned-map response is immaterial for a documented nuisance, a
   163	  frozen-map-only treatment is allowed only with the proof and bound in its
   164	  component summary.
   165	- If material for selected bands, expand those complete bands/endpoints.
   166	- If broadly material, expand to the full applicable universe inventory.
   167	
   168	This gate controls cost, not estimator definition: every adopted endpoint is
   169	still a complete joint variation, and every universe repeats the literal
   170	background injection plus Stay-Positive construction.
   171	
   172	## Gate 7 — full-schema selection-complete laterals
   173	
   174	Starts only after G2 and a fresh P3F-PET campaign using the same full schema.
   175	For every declared lateral endpoint, rebuild selection, event membership,
   176	background target, native misses, full-event inputs, retrained mapping, and
   177	extraction together.
   178	
   179	P3S standard endpoints, scalar-FPS purity unfolds, and reduced-schema P3F
   180	artifacts are controls only. Every accepted endpoint must share the nominal
   181	estimator fingerprint and differ only by the declared physical variation.
   182	Build the lateral component from the complete asymmetric endpoint inventory
   183	under the experiment's declared centering convention, with a separate mean
   184	shift record.
   185	
   186	## Gate 8 — adoption and exact projections
   187	
   188	Declare nuisance ownership and independence/coupling before covariance
   189	assembly. On one matched central/mask/order/fingerprint, assemble only:
   190	
   191	```text
   192	C_total = C_stat + C_ML + C_syst_joint + C_lateral_joint
   193	```
   194	
   195	There is no separate additive `C_retrain`. Require exact component/block
   196	reconstruction, common central and ordering, symmetry, PSD/eigen diagnostics,
   197	finite diagonal, mean-shift diagnostics, extended-edge validation, and exact
   198	5D-to-4D covariance projection.
   199	
   200	Adoption also requires acceptance-supported/prior-dominated tier outputs,
   201	coverage and prior-envelope controls, scalar/PET comparison only on a common
   202	measurement domain, tracked product summary, and ledger/RUN_LOG/STATUS entries
   203	in the same commit.
   204	
   205	Until Gate 8 lands, label outputs by component and candidate fingerprint; do
   206	not call them the final PET budget.
   207	
   208	## Reuse/rerun matrix
   209	
   210	| Item | Disposition |
   211	|---|---|
   212	| Recoil-only PET products and copied scalar purity targets | `QUARANTINE` cross-check; no transfer |
   213	| Reduced full-event interface/stress products | `QUARANTINE` interface cross-check; no transfer |
   214	| Extended-edge and reporting conventions | `REUSE` after exact contract/hash validation |
   215	| Raw source AnaTuple inputs and per-playlist manifests | `REUSE` after provenance/availability checks |
   216	| Existing FPS event-loop roots lacking full schema/background clouds | Insufficient for G2; do not relabel |
   217	| G2 full-schema FPS CV roots/NPZ | `BUILD` once after binary drain/ownership gate |
   218	| Literal signed/refined nominal target | `BUILD` from G2 inventories |
   219	| Publication nominal and GPU floor | `BUILD` under one fingerprint |
   220	| `C_stat` | `BUILD` with F7 full data/signal/background draws |
   221	| PET `C_ML` | `BUILD` independently with no Poisson |
   222	| Vertical/flux systematic component | `BUILD` from adopted joint end-to-end universes |
   223	| P3F-PET laterals | `BUILD` after G2 with full-schema shifted inputs |
   224	| Full per-universe retraining | `GATED` by targeted materiality, never assumed unnecessary |
   225	
   226	## Atomicity and scheduler gate
   227	
   228	Every unit writes a unique temporary artifact, runs its content validator, and
   229	atomically renames only on success. Resume logic checks manifest completeness,
   230	schema/fingerprint, and content—not file size. Partial artifacts are
   231	quarantined.
   232	
   233	Before each launch, inspect existing allocations and queues. Use an owner-held
   234	interactive node for single-node work that fits its remaining wall; queue
   235	dependency-safe arrays, long/GPU jobs, or work that must outlive the shell early.
   236	Never duplicate a queued/running writer, and never cancel another worker's job
   237	or allocation.

# BLOCK

## Rank 1 — P3F-PET still does not precede the publication nominal

The repair claims fresh full-schema P3F-PET is required before the nominal, but the controls still permit parallel execution:

- P5A prerequisites include PG0 and G2, but not a committed P3F-PET source ([runbook line 185](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/PUBLICATION_COMPLETION_RUNBOOK.md:185)).
- The dependency graph branches G2 directly to both the nominal and P3F-PET, with no `P3FP → NPET` edge ([rerun map line 40](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/RESULT_DEPENDENCY_AND_RERUN_MAP.md:40)).
- The PET DAG trains the nominal at Gate 3, while fresh P3F-PET appears only at Gate 7 ([PET DAG line 92](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:92), [line 172](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_REMEDIATION_STATUS.md:172)).

This contradicts the authoritative requirement that G3/P3F endpoints are committed and gate-passed before any P5B launch ([feature contract line 204](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/nd-unfolding/pet/FULL_EVENT_FEATURE_CONTRACT.md:204)) and current takeover ordering, where the nominal/UQ chain requires T7 ([takeover status line 22](/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold/docs/orchestration/MIGRATION-TAKEOVER-STATUS.md:22)).

Smallest correction:

- Add the committed P3F-PET source manifest to P5A prerequisites.
- Add `P3FP → NPET` to the dependency graph.
- Split PET Gate 7: move full-schema P3F-PET source production and validation before Gate 3; retain endpoint retraining/covariance construction after the nominal.
- Before nominal launch, require the exact `5 bands × 2 endpoints × 12 playlists` inventory, event/cloud/background joins, migration census, hashes, schema identity, and commit receipt.

**Classification: blocks committing the plan.**

All other eight prior defects are resolved.
